mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth expands corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 growth on 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window, or average 60-day returns during expansion periods are not higher than during contractions (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after trigger are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or hit rate does not exceed 55% out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low policy rates combined with high housing starts indicate strong monetary accommodation and robust residential investment, jointly lowering discount rates and boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: Joint trigger produces negative or insignificant 20-day forward returns (Rank IC <= 0) in at least 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term in a regression is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production grows strongly year-over-year, it signals robust manufacturing activity and economic momentum, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during triggered periods are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or show negative average returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand and income growth, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth on 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the long-short spread from top vs bottom decile is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in inflation above a critical threshold forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after trigger are not negative on average (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate for negative returns is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: A very tight labor market, characterized by low unemployment and strong payroll growth, signals wage pressures and high consumer confidence, which supports equity prices over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during triggered periods are positive in less than 55% of cases, or the average return is not positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll growth indicates a strengthening labor market with rising incomes and spending, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during payroll acceleration periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
